content:
1	---
2	name: blueprint-writer
3	description: Update one blueprint chapter to reflect strategy changes, fill missing definitions or theorems, or align prose with the current Lean structure. Plan-agent-dispatched; one writer per chapter. May spawn reference-retriever mid-session when drafting reveals a missing source.
4	write_domain: "blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: true
7	default_enabled: false
8	dispatcher_notes: |
9	  - Dispatch one writer per chapter that the most recent blueprint
10	    review (the blueprint-review subagent in your catalog, when
11	    present) flagged as incomplete, lacking proof detail, or missing
12	    multi-route coverage.
13	  - Each directive must be precise: strategy context (the slice that
14	    matters for this chapter), required definitions/theorems (with
15	    enough mathematical detail to formalize), references, and
16	    explicit out-of-scope items. Writers do NOT speculate beyond
17	    what the directive lists.
18	  - **NEVER instruct the writer to add ``\leanok`` or ``\mathlibok``
19	    markers** (no "after writing the block, add `\leanok`"; no
20	    "verification: confirm `\leanok` is present"). The writer's
21	    descriptor forbids it — ``\leanok`` is managed by the
22	    deterministic ``sync_leanok`` phase, ``\mathlibok`` by the review
23	    agent. A directive that asks the writer to add markers puts it in
24	    a rule conflict; the writer must obey its descriptor, so the
25	    "verification" instruction will appear to fail until sync_leanok
26	    runs. Leave marker concerns out of the writer's directive entirely.
27	  - **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If
28	    the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the
29	    writer with TWO globs:
30	      --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'
31	      --write-domain 'references/**'
32	    The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt
33	    body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child
34	    reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit
35	    `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not
36	    need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).
37	  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters
38	    are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to
39	    parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a
40	    route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.
41	  - After a significant writer round, re-run the blueprint review
42	    in the same iteration (the relevant entry in your catalog) to
43	    confirm the updated blueprint is now sufficient — do not assume
44	    the writer fixed everything.
45	  - If a writer's report contains entries under "Strategy-modifying
46	    findings", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean
47	    work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a
48	    strategy-level issue.
49	  - If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,
50	    skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the
51	    writer's output — the writer relied on them, so should you.
52	---
53	
54	# Blueprint Writer
55	
56	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
57	
58	## Your Job
59	
60	The plan agent has decided that a specific blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`) needs to change — to reflect a strategic decision, fill a missing definition or theorem the project needs, or align the informal prose with the current Lean structure. Your directive tells you which chapter, what must be there, and what counts as out-of-scope.
61	
62	You only edit the **one chapter named in your directive**. Your declared `--write-domain` should reflect that (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`). You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.
63	
64	## Directive Format
65	
66	```markdown
67	# Blueprint Writer Directive
68	
69	## Slug
70	<slug>
71	
72	## Target chapter
73	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
74	
75	## Strategy context
76	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted for you — typically a paragraph or two describing what this chapter must support in the overall arc. You do NOT read STRATEGY.md yourself; the plan agent gives you only what is relevant.>
77	
78	## Required content
79	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
80	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
81	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
82	- ...
83	
84	## Out of scope
85	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
86	
87	## References
88	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
89	- ...
90	
91	## Expected outcome
92	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
93	```
94	
95	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.
96	
97	## Chapter format
98	
99	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
100	
101	```latex
102	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
103	  \label{thm:some_label}
104	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
105	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
106	  Informal statement of the theorem, using standard mathematical notation.
107	\end{theorem}
108	
109	\begin{proof}
110	  \uses{thm:another_result}
111	  Step-by-step informal proof. Reference blueprint labels with \uses{...}
112	  so the dependency graph stays accurate.
113	\end{proof}
114	```
115	
116	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
117	
118	## Rules
119	
120	### What you CAN do
121	- Add new declaration blocks (definitions, lemmas, theorems, propositions, corollaries) under direction.
122	- Expand or revise existing prose / proof sketches in your assigned chapter.
123	- Add `\uses{...}` cross-references.
124	- Adjust `\lean{...}` hints when the directive names a new Lean target.
125	- Read `references/summary.md` and any reference that is in `references/` to ground your writing in the project's sources.
126	
127	### What you MUST do
128	- **Keep the chapter valid LaTeX.** Don't leave dangling `\begin{...}` without matching `\end{...}`. Compile-checking is the plan agent's responsibility but you must not introduce syntax errors.
129	- **Stay within your chapter.** Your declared write-domain is one `*.tex` file. The Archon CLI rejects writes outside it.
130	- **Define non-standard macros in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`** before using them — but: that file is outside your write-domain, so you DON'T touch it. If the directive requires a new macro, you note in your report "needs macro `\foo`" and leave the LaTeX using the new command name; the plan agent adds the macro before next iter's typeset.
131	- **Use mathematical, not Lean-syntactic prose.** Describe the proof in the language of mathematics — definitions, set inclusions, ring maps, universal properties — not in Lean tactic syntax. The prover formalizes your math.
132	- **Document every change** in your report.
133	
134	### What you MUST NOT do
135	- **Do NOT add `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers.** Those are managed by the `sync_leanok` phase + the review agent — never by you.
136	- **Do NOT edit other chapters.** Even when you spot a related issue, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" instead of fixing it.
137	- **Do NOT edit `content.tex`** (the top-level blueprint file that `\input`s the chapters).
138	- **Do NOT edit `.lean` files** or any other state file.
139	- **Do NOT write Lean syntax** — keep the chapter mathematical, not syntactic.
140	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
141	
142	## Reading references
143	
144	You write mathematics, not literature criticism — your prose must be grounded in authoritative sources, not your training memory. Before composing any new declaration block:
145	
146	1. **Read `references/summary.md`** to see the index of sources the project already has. Sources directly relevant to your chapter are your first stop.
147	2. **Read every reference file your directive names** under its `## References` section. The plan agent named those because the chapter needs them.
148	3. **Read anything in `references/summary.md` your directive didn't name but that is clearly relevant** to your chapter (same area, same theorem, same construction).
149	
150	Do NOT write content from your training memory when you could ground it in a reference instead. When the references say one thing and your memory says another, trust the references.
151	
152	## Dispatching a reference-retriever (when the project's sources don't have what you need)
153	
154	If, while drafting, you discover that the chapter needs material the project's `references/` doesn't contain, **dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** rather than guessing or papering over the gap.
155	
156	Conditions for dispatch:
157	
158	- The directive named a source (`Smith 2018`, an arXiv ID) that isn't in `references/` yet.
159	- The required content is in a known textbook (Hartshorne, Vakil, Stacks Project, etc.) but the project hasn't summarized the relevant chapter.
160	- You need a Mathlib-adjacent source (nLab, math overflow, Stacks Project) the directive didn't anticipate.
161	
162	Dispatch (Bash, foreground, in your write-domain only if it includes `references/**`):
163	
164	```
165	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
166	  --name reference-retriever \
167	  --slug <kebab-slug-for-the-source> \
168	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/reference-retriever-<child-slug>-directive.md \
169	  --write-domain 'references/**'
170	```
171	
172	Write the directive file first; the directive format is documented in `.archon/subagents/reference-retriever.md`. The retriever returns when the new summary is on disk; **then resume writing**, citing the new reference.
173	
174	If your invocation's recorded write-domain does NOT include `references/**` (your `parent.write_domain` in `dispatch.jsonl`), the dispatch will be rejected. In that case, STOP writing the affected section, report the missing source in "Notes for Plan Agent", and finish the parts of the chapter you can still write. The plan agent will re-dispatch you next iter with the broader write-domain.
175	
176	## Workflow
177	
178	1. Read your directive completely.
179	2. **Read `references/summary.md`** and every reference your directive points at; also read sibling-chapter material that informs cross-references.
180	3. Read the target chapter currently on disk to see what's already there.
181	4. Plan the edits: which blocks to add, which to revise. Decide where each new block goes in the chapter's existing flow.
182	5. **If a needed source is missing from `references/`, dispatch a `reference-retriever`** (see above) and wait for it to return before drafting the affected sections.
183	6. Make the edits.
184	7. Verify the file is still valid LaTeX at a glance (no unmatched begin/end, balanced braces in `\label`/`\uses`/`\lean`).
185	8. Write your report.
186	
187	## Logging
188	
189	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
190	
191	```markdown
192	# Blueprint Writer Report
193	
194	## Slug
195	<slug>
196	
197	## Status
198	<COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE>
199	<If INCOMPLETE: which required items could not be written and why.>
200	
201	## Target chapter
202	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
203	
204	## Changes Made
205	- **Added definition** `\definition`/`\label{def:foo}`/`\lean{Foo.bar}` — <one line on what it captures>
206	- **Added theorem** `\theorem`/`\label{thm:foo}` — <one line on statement>
207	  - Proof sketch added: <Y/N + brief shape>
208	- **Revised** `<existing label>` — <one line on what changed>
209	- ...
210	
211	## Cross-references introduced
212	- `\uses{thm:bar}` added in proof of `\thm:foo` — verify `thm:bar` exists in <chapter or this same one>
213	- ...
214	
215	## Macros needed (if any)
216	- `\foo{...}` — used in <where>; needs definition in `macros/common.tex`. NOT added by me (out of write-domain).
217	
218	## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)
219	- slug `<child-slug>`: requested `<source>`. Status: COMPLETE / NOT_FOUND / PARTIAL. Summary at `references/<slug>.md`.
220	- ...
221	
222	## Notes for Plan Agent
223	- <any inconsistency I noticed in sibling chapters that the directive didn't cover>
224	- <any structural concern: e.g. "the chapter is now 800 lines, consider splitting">
225	- <any reference I had to guess at because the directive's reference section was incomplete>
226	
227	## Strategy-modifying findings
228	<populate this section ONLY if writing the chapter surfaced a need to
229	change the project's strategy itself — not just the chapter content.
230	Examples:
231	- A definition you were asked to write turns out to require a property
232	  that the strategy assumes is automatic but is not.
233	- A theorem you sketched is provable as stated, but its consequence
234	  used elsewhere in STRATEGY.md does not follow from it.
235	- A reference cited as authoritative actually requires a different
236	  setup than the strategy assumes.
237	
238	When this section is non-empty, the plan agent must update STRATEGY.md
239	before any further Lean work this iteration. Do NOT silently adapt the
240	chapter to paper over a strategy-level issue — surface it here.>
241	```
242	
243	## Return Value
244	
245	Your final assistant message:
246	
247	- One line: `<slug>: COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE — <one-sentence outcome>`
248	- The path to your full report.
249	
250	Keep the inline return short. The plan agent reads the full report.
251

1	\chapter{Cotangent space at the identity (piece (i) — Lean file)}
2	\label{chap:cotangent-grpobj}
3	
4	This chapter is a pointer to the Lean file
5	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean}. The mathematical content
6	itself lives in chapter~\ref{chap:RigidityKbar} (\texttt{RigidityKbar.tex}
7	\S "Piece (i)") and is not duplicated here. This chapter exists to satisfy
8	the per-Lean-file blueprint convention (one chapter per Lean file).
9	
10	\textbf{Iter-144 chart-algebra pivot disposition.} The bundled-route
11	closure path (pieces (i.b) + (i.c) via global shear-iso globalisation +
12	sheaf-of-modules base-change) is \textbf{DESCOPED iter-145+}; this
13	file's remaining \texttt{sorry}-bodied declarations are preserved as
14	auditable record of the bundled route. The iter-145+ M2.body-pile prover
15	work targets the chart-algebra route, primarily living in a new piece
16	(ii) inflation (see \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} \S "Piece (ii)" iter-144
17	chart-algebra envelope in \texttt{RigidityKbar.tex}).
18	% NOTE: iter-145 review — paragraph is structurally inaccurate post the iter-145 Q7 refactor (`refactor-chart-algebra-skeleton-bundled-excise-iter145` excised 3 named targets + 2 cascade-excised consumers totalling 272 LOC). After the excise, ZERO sorry-bodied declarations remain in `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`. The five `\item`s below describing the excised declarations are now manifest-drifted: they describe Lean state that no longer exists. The audit record is preserved in (a) git history of `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`, (b) the `analogies/` design notes, and (c) `iter/iter-145/review.md` + `proof-journal/sessions/session_145/`. A blueprint-writer dispatch is queued in `proof-journal/sessions/session_145/recommendations.md` (recommendation #2) to rewrite this paragraph + the five affected `\item`s with `EXCISED iter-145` dispositions; iter-146+ HARD GATE on `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean` until that writer pass lands.
19	
20	\section{Lean declarations in this file}
21	
22	\begin{itemize}
23	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity}
24	        --- the definition of the cotangent space at the identity as a
25	        \texttt{ModuleCat k}. Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}
26	        (piece (i.a); closed iter-128, body refactored through iter-131).
27	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_eq\_extendScalars}
28	        --- structural-shape acceptance lemma (piece (i.a); closed
29	        iter-131; supports both \texttt{change}-based and
30	        \texttt{obtain}+\texttt{rw} rewrite patterns downstream).
31	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_finrank\_eq}
32	        --- rank lemma (piece (i.a); closed iter-132).
33	        Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}.
34	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.shearMulRight} --- the binary-product
35	        shear isomorphism $\sigma = \langle \pr_1, \mu \rangle$ in
36	        $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ (piece (i.b) Step 1; iter-134
37	        \texttt{NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL}, $\sim 30$--$60$ LOC; \emph{closed}
38	        iter-134). Built from \texttt{GrpObj} data only; modelled on
39	        \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight}. Companion \texttt{@[simps]}
40	        lemmas \texttt{shearMulRight\_hom\_fst} and
41	        \texttt{shearMulRight\_hom\_snd} expose the first/second-coordinate
42	        identifications $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_1 = \pr_1$ and
43	        $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_2 = \mu$.
44	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.schemeHomRingCompatibility} ---
45	        packaging helper around $((\mathrm{adj}).\mathrm{homEquiv}\,\_\,\_).\mathrm{symm}\,f.c$,
46	        the adjunction-transpose compatibility morphism of structure presheaves
47	        of rings along a scheme morphism $f \colon Y \to Z$. Used by
48	        \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}. Note: this is \emph{not} the
49	        $\varphi$ for \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}, which expects a
50	        morphism of presheaves on the codomain $Y$, obtained via Mathlib's
51	        \texttt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom} (iter-135 mathlib-analogist
52	        verdict; see \texttt{analogies/phi-compatibility-morphisms.md}).
53	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_basechange\_along\_proj\_two}
54	        --- presheaf-of-modules base-change helper (piece (i.b) Step 2;
55	        iter-134+ NEEDS\_MATHLIB\_GAP\_FILL, $\sim 150$--$300$ LOC).
56	        Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj}. Iter-138 prover
57	        landed PARTIAL with substantive Route (b) skeleton; three concrete
58	        sub-sorries remain inside this declaration's body and inside the
59	        two helpers below (iter-140 prover targets).
60	        % NOTE: iter-145 review — declaration EXCISED iter-145 (cascade-excise; the body referenced the excised `basechange_along_proj_two_inv` + `basechange_along_proj_two_inv_app_isIso`). NOT preserved in tree. Prose above describes pre-excise state; the iter-145 EXCISE breadcrumb at `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:552–560` records the deletion. Rewrite recipe pending blueprint-writer dispatch (see iter-145 recommendations.md #2).
61	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv\_derivation}
62	        --- pointwise inverse-direction derivation $D$ on the transparent
63	        pushforward of $\Omega_{(G\times_k G)/G}$ along the second-projection
64	        compatibility morphism, defined via
65	        \texttt{PresheafOfModules.Derivation'.mk} (piece (i.b) Step 2,
66	        iter-138 helper). Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj_inv_derivation}.
67	        The additive ($d_{\mathrm{add}}$) and Leibniz ($d_{\mathrm{mul}}$)
68	        laws closed iter-138; the $d_{\map}$ (cross-open naturality) law
69	        closed iter-142 via the 3-step \texttt{ALIGN\_WITH\_MATHLIB} chase
70	        (\texttt{pushforward\_obj\_map\_apply'} +
71	        \texttt{NatTrans.naturality\_apply} +
72	        \texttt{relativeDifferentials'\_map\_d}); the $d_{\app}$ (zero on
73	        $\varphi_G$-image) law remains a \texttt{sorry}-bodied sub-goal
74	        (the step-3.a categorical equality
75	        $(\fst\,G\,G).\mathrm{left} \circ G.\mathrm{hom} =
76	        (\snd\,G\,G).\mathrm{left} \circ G.\mathrm{hom}$ landed iter-143
77	        as a 1-LOC \texttt{rw}; the residual 3.b $\to$ 3.d chase is
78	        blocked at the \texttt{Pushforward.comp\_eq} + \texttt{eqToHom}
79	        type-coercion layer). \textbf{DESCOPED iter-145+ under the
80	        iter-144 chart-algebra pivot}; preserved in-tree as auditable
81	        record of the bundled route.
82	        % NOTE: iter-145 review — declaration EXCISED iter-145 per strategy-critic-iter145 Q7 sunk-cost ruling; NOT preserved in tree. Audit record in git history of `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean` + `analogies/d-app-d-map-recipe-shape.md`.
83	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv}
84	        --- inverse morphism
85	        $(\PresheafOfModules.pullback\,\psi).\obj\,\Omega_{G/k} \to \Omega_{(G\times_k G)/G}$
86	        for the base-change iso, obtained by transposing the universal-
87	        property-derived morphism through the
88	        \texttt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction} (piece (i.b) Step 2,
89	        iter-138 helper). Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj_inv}.
90	        Sorry-free in Lean iter-138. The \texttt{IsIso} obligation was
91	        extracted iter-143 (Wave 2 refactor;
92	        \texttt{refactor-isiso-extract-iter143}) into a new top-level
93	        named theorem \texttt{basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv\_app\_isIso}
94	        (see next item) to satisfy the iter-143 \texttt{STRATEGY.md}
95	        Edit 1 sorry-must-be-named-declaration discipline. The consuming
96	        \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_basechange\_along\_proj\_two}
97	        declaration now invokes the named theorem via
98	        \texttt{isIso\_of\_app\_iso\_module} rather than an inline
99	        \texttt{(fun \_ => sorry)}. \textbf{DESCOPED iter-145+ under the
100	        iter-144 chart-algebra pivot}.
101	        % NOTE: iter-145 review — declaration EXCISED iter-145 (cascade-excise; referenced the excised `_inv_derivation` + `_inv_app_isIso`). NOT preserved in tree.
102	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv\_app\_isIso}
103	        --- per-open \texttt{IsIso} obligation extracted iter-143
104	        (Wave 2 refactor) from a
105	        \texttt{letI := \ldots\ (fun \_ => sorry)} pattern inside
106	        \texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_basechange\_along\_proj\_two}.
107	        Body is \texttt{sorry}; the closure recipe is Route (b'2)
108	        items 2--4 documented at
109	        \cref{lem:GrpObj_basechange_along_proj_two_inv_app_isIso}
110	        in \texttt{RigidityKbar.tex} (the first-class blueprint block
111	        added iter-144 by the
112	        \texttt{blueprint-writer-rigiditykbar-iter144} dispatch).
113	        \textbf{DESCOPED iter-145+ under the iter-144 chart-algebra
114	        pivot}; preserved as auditable record.
115	        % NOTE: iter-145 review — declaration EXCISED iter-145 per strategy-critic-iter145 Q7 sunk-cost ruling; NOT preserved in tree.
116	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_restrict\_along\_identity\_section}
117	        --- section-restriction helper (piece (i.b) Step 3; iter-134+,
118	        $\sim 30$--$80$ LOC).
119	        Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section}.
120	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent}
121	        --- main piece (i.b) trivialisation lemma; presheaf-of-modules
122	        level statement; sheaf-level RHS per the iter-133 mathlib-analogist
123	        Decision 4 and iter-134 plan-agent pre-commitment.
124	        Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises}.
125	        \textbf{Iter-145+ DESCOPED under the iter-144 chart-algebra
126	        pivot}; the bundled-route Main composition target is no longer
127	        on the M2.body-pile critical path. Body remains \texttt{sorry}
128	        as auditable record; the chart-algebra route bypasses the
129	        global shear-iso globalisation entirely via per-chart
130	        \texttt{Algebra.IsPushout} + per-chart Kähler derivation.
131	        % NOTE: iter-145 review — declaration EXCISED iter-145 per strategy-critic-iter145 Q7 sunk-cost ruling; body is NOT `sorry`-as-auditable-record but DELETED. Audit record in git history of `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean` + `analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md` (if present) + `analogies/cotangent-body-shape.md`. Functional replacement: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` 5-piece decomposition (iter-146+ closure path).
132	\end{itemize}
133	
134	For the informal mathematical content + closure-path documentation, see
135	\cref{chap:RigidityKbar} \S "Piece (i)" in \texttt{RigidityKbar.tex}.
136

159:lemmas (not yet in this file); the structural-shape accessibility needed
162:noncomputable def cotangentSpaceAtIdentity (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
211:theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
257:theorem cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
349:@[simps]
350:def shearMulRight (G : C) [GrpObj G] : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G where
386:@[reassoc (attr := simp)]

input.content:
\chapter{Cotangent space at the identity (piece (i) --- Lean file)}
\label{chap:cotangent-grpobj}

This chapter is a pointer to the Lean file
\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean}. The mathematical content
itself lives in chapter~\ref{chap:RigidityKbar} (\texttt{RigidityKbar.tex}
\S "Piece (i)") and is not duplicated here. This chapter exists to satisfy
the per-Lean-file blueprint convention (one chapter per Lean file).

\textbf{Post-iter-145 disposition.} After the iter-145 refactor
\texttt{refactor-chart-algebra-skeleton-bundled-excise-iter145}, the
file contains the piece (i.a) trio
(\texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity},
\texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_eq\_extendScalars},
\texttt{cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_finrank\_eq}, all closed
iter-128--iter-132) plus a small set of orphan helpers from the
iter-134--iter-136 bundled-route build
(\texttt{shearMulRight} with its \texttt{\_hom\_fst}/\texttt{\_hom\_snd}
companions, \texttt{schemeHomRingCompatibility}, and
\texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_restrict\_along\_identity\_section}),
together with two private structural helpers
(\texttt{section\_snd\_eq\_identity\_struct} and
\texttt{isIso\_of\_app\_iso\_module}) that the surviving public
declarations use locally. Zero \texttt{sorry}-bodied declarations
remain in this file. The five excised bundled-route declarations
(\texttt{basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv\_derivation},
\texttt{basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv},
\texttt{basechange\_along\_proj\_two\_inv\_app\_isIso},
\texttt{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_basechange\_along\_proj\_two},
\texttt{mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent}) and their iter-138--iter-143
build history live in the git history of this file (per the iter-145
Q7 "auditable record IS git history" framing in \texttt{STRATEGY.md}
\S Iter-144 chart-algebra pivot). The active chart-algebra piece (ii)
work has moved to \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean}
with informal content in \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} \S "Chart-algebra
piece (ii) first-class decomposition" of \texttt{RigidityKbar.tex}.

\section{Lean declarations in this file}

\begin{itemize}
  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity}
        --- the definition of the cotangent space at the identity as a
        \texttt{ModuleCat k}. Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}
        (piece (i.a); closed iter-128, body refactored through iter-131).
  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_eq\_extendScalars}
        --- structural-shape acceptance lemma (piece (i.a); closed
        iter-131; supports both \texttt{change}-based and
        \texttt{obtain}+\texttt{rw} rewrite patterns downstream).
  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity\_finrank\_eq}
        --- rank lemma (piece (i.a); closed iter-132).
        Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}.
  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.shearMulRight} --- the binary-product
        shear isomorphism $\sigma = \langle \pr_1, \mu \rangle$ in
        $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ (piece (i.b) Step 1; closed iter-134). Built
        from \texttt{GrpObj} data only; modelled on
        \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight}. Companion \texttt{@[simps]}
        lemmas \texttt{shearMulRight\_hom\_fst} and
        \texttt{shearMulRight\_hom\_snd} expose the first/second-coordinate
        identifications $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_1 = \pr_1$ and
        $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_2 = \mu$. Closed iter-134;
        consumer (piece (i.b) Step 2 chain) DELETED iter-145 under the
        chart-algebra pivot, leaving these as standalone categorical
        helpers. Iter-146+ cleanup candidate; preserved iter-145 per the
        refactor directive's \texttt{private}-or-public-discipline rule.
  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.schemeHomRingCompatibility} ---
        packaging helper around $((\mathrm{adj}).\mathrm{homEquiv}\,\_\,\_).\mathrm{symm}\,f.c$,
        the adjunction-transpose compatibility morphism of structure presheaves
        of rings along a scheme morphism $f \colon Y \to Z$. Note: this is
        \emph{not} the $\varphi$ for \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback},
        which expects a morphism of presheaves on the codomain $Y$,
        obtained via Mathlib's \texttt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}
        (iter-135 mathlib-analogist verdict; see
        \texttt{analogies/phi-compatibility-morphisms.md}). Closed
        iter-135; consumer (piece (i.b) Step 2 chain) DELETED iter-145
        under the chart-algebra pivot, leaving this as a standalone
        adjunction-transpose utility. Iter-146+ cleanup candidate.
  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf\_restrict\_along\_identity\_section}
        --- section-restriction helper (piece (i.b) Step 3).
        Statement~\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section}.
        Closed iter-136; consumer (the Compose main lemma
        \texttt{mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent}) DELETED iter-145 under
        the chart-algebra pivot, leaving this as a standalone Step 3
        section-restriction utility. Iter-146+ cleanup candidate.
\end{itemize}

For the informal mathematical content + closure-path documentation, see
\cref{chap:RigidityKbar} \S "Piece (i)" in \texttt{RigidityKbar.tex}.

# Blueprint Writer Report

## Slug
pointer-iter146

## Status
COMPLETE

## Target chapter
blueprint/src/chapters/AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_GrpObj.tex

## Changes Made

- **Rewrote disposition paragraph** (was L10–L18, the
  "Iter-144 chart-algebra pivot disposition" block + its long
  iter-145-review `% NOTE:` annotation).
  - Old text claimed "this file's remaining `sorry`-bodied declarations
    are preserved as auditable record of the bundled route" — structurally
    wrong post-iter-145, when zero `sorry`-bodied declarations remain.
  - New text describes post-iter-145 reality across four pieces of
    prose: (1) piece (i.a) trio in-tree, (2) orphan helpers in-tree
    (`shearMulRight` family + `schemeHomRingCompatibility` +
    `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section`),
    plus the two private helpers (`section_snd_eq_identity_struct`
    and `isIso_of_app_iso_module`) acknowledged as in-tree but not
    surfaced as their own `\item`s; (3) five excised bundled-route
    declarations named, with the iter-145 Q7 "git history IS the audit
    record" framing; (4) pointer to `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean`
    + `RigidityKbar.tex` "Chart-algebra piece (ii) first-class
    decomposition" subsection for the active piece (ii) work.

- **Deleted 5 stale `\item` bullets outright** (former lines ~52–131):
  - `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`
  - `basechange_along_proj_two_inv_derivation`
  - `basechange_along_proj_two_inv`
  - `basechange_along_proj_two_inv_app_isIso`
  - `mulRight_globalises_cotangent`
  No `EXCISED iter-145` placeholder bullets carried forward — per the
  directive, the audit record is git history.

- **Revised 3 surviving orphan-helper bullets** with a one-sentence
  honest orphan-status flag + iter-146+ cleanup-candidate label:
  - `shearMulRight` + `_hom_fst`/`_hom_snd` (added: "Closed iter-134;
    consumer DELETED iter-145…; iter-146+ cleanup candidate; preserved
    iter-145 per refactor directive's `private`-or-public-discipline
    rule"). Also dropped the now-misleading "NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL,
    ~30–60 LOC" pre-closure envelope phrasing.
  - `schemeHomRingCompatibility` (added: "Closed iter-135;
    consumer DELETED iter-145…; iter-146+ cleanup candidate"). Also
    dropped the dangling "Used by `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf`"
    framing, since the referenced declaration no longer exists.
  - `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section`
    (added: "Closed iter-136; consumer (`mulRight_globalises_cotangent`)
    DELETED iter-145…; iter-146+ cleanup candidate"). Dropped the
    "iter-134+, ~30–80 LOC" pre-closure envelope.

- **Left 3 piece (i.a) bullets verbatim** per the directive's "NO
  CHANGE" guidance:
  - `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity`
  - `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_eq_extendScalars`
  - `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq`

- **Preserved verbatim**:
  - Chapter `\chapter{...}` heading + `\label{chap:cotangent-grpobj}`
  - First paragraph identifying the chapter as a pointer to
    `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`
  - `\section{Lean declarations in this file}` heading + `itemize`
    environment scaffolding
  - Closing pointer paragraph to `\cref{chap:RigidityKbar} \S "Piece (i)"`

## Cross-references introduced

No new `\uses{}` cross-references; this chapter is a pointer chapter
and uses `\cref` only.

Existing `\cref` links retained:
- `\cref{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace}` (in `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` bullet)
- `\cref{lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank}` (in `_finrank_eq` bullet)
- `\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section}` (in
  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section` bullet)
- `\cref{chap:RigidityKbar}` (closing paragraph + new disposition paragraph)

Removed `\cref` links (because the referenced `\item` bullets were
deleted):
- `\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj}` (was in
  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two` bullet)
- `\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj_inv_derivation}` (was in
  `basechange_along_proj_two_inv_derivation` bullet)
- `\cref{lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj_inv}` (was in
  `basechange_along_proj_two_inv` bullet)
- `\cref{lem:GrpObj_basechange_along_proj_two_inv_app_isIso}` (was in
  `basechange_along_proj_two_inv_app_isIso` bullet)
- `\cref{lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises}` (was in
  `mulRight_globalises_cotangent` bullet)

These five removed `\cref` links continue to point at blueprint blocks
that *do* still exist in `RigidityKbar.tex` (the directive said the
chart-algebra-content-in-RigidityKbar-subsection routing was clean and
out-of-scope to touch); they are just no longer cited from this
pointer chapter. The next blueprint-doctor run should confirm the
five lemma labels remain reachable from somewhere else in
`RigidityKbar.tex`; if any has become an orphan target, the plan agent
may want to dispatch a separate review-side pass against
`RigidityKbar.tex`. (Flagged below under Notes for Plan Agent.)

## Macros needed (if any)
None. The chapter uses only standard macros (`\texttt`, `\cref`,
`\Spec`, `\Over`, `\pr`, `\fst`, `\snd`, etc.) already in
`macros/common.tex`. The removed bullets also used these same
macros plus `\mathrm` / `\circ` from base LaTeX.

## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)
None — the directive's scope is purely a pointer-chapter manifest
reconciliation; the mathematical content for the active chart-algebra
piece (ii) lives in `RigidityKbar.tex` and is out-of-scope per
directive.

## Notes for Plan Agent

- **LOC delta achieved:** chapter went from 136 LOC to 87 LOC
  (−49 LOC). The directive's expected envelope was ~−82 LOC to a
  ~50-LOC final; the actual delta lands somewhat above that target
  because I retained the orphan-status sentences as standalone
  prose (rather than collapsing them into the existing `\item`
  body in a tighter shape). The chapter is still ~35% smaller than
  pre-edit and structurally accurate; if the plan agent wants a
  further tighter pass, it would compress the three orphan
  bullets to ~2 lines each (currently ~6 each). Not done in this
  pass since the directive said "light prose touch" rather than
  "compress aggressively".

- **Five removed `\cref` targets** (listed above under
  "Cross-references introduced → Removed") may now have lower
  inbound-link counts in `RigidityKbar.tex`. They were not the
  only inbound links (the directive notes the chart-algebra
  subsection at L1831–L1956 of `RigidityKbar.tex` is the active
  consumer side), but a blueprint-doctor confirmation that the
  five bundled-route lemma labels remain reachable would be
  prudent. If any has become an orphan label, the plan agent may
  want to dispatch a `RigidityKbar.tex`-side blueprint-writer to
  either re-cite them or descope them with `% EXCISED iter-145`
  comments per the project's audit-trail conventions.

- **Two private helpers acknowledged in prose, no `\item` for them.**
  Per the directive's explicit guidance (and per the iter-145
  lean-vs-blueprint-checker recommendation), I named the two
  private helpers (`section_snd_eq_identity_struct`,
  `isIso_of_app_iso_module`) inside the disposition paragraph as
  "two private structural helpers … that the surviving public
  declarations use locally" rather than minting separate `\item`
  bullets for them. This matches the directive ("DO NOT introduce
  a separate `\item` for the two private helpers").

- **No `\leanok` / `\mathlibok` markers touched** (per the
  blueprint-writer descriptor's hard rule; the deterministic
  `sync_leanok` phase + review-agent semantic pass own these).

## Strategy-modifying findings

None. The chapter rewrite is a pure manifest-reconciliation pass:
text now matches Lean-file state. No strategy-level issue surfaced
during the rewrite — the iter-145 chart-algebra-pivot strategy +
the iter-145 Q7 "git history IS the audit record" framing
correctly support the manifest-drift fix this directive prescribes.
